trivia_bullets.text: Celestial Enforcer is an M21 original — not a reprint. It is a Human Cleric (1WW, 2/3) first printed in Core Set 2021 (July 2020) and has not received additional printings as of May 2026. [Scryfall]

Flavor text (no attributed speaker): "I look to the heavens for hope, but you will kneel." The line pairs devotion with enforcement — the figure's piety is personal while the coercion is directed outward. [Scryfall]

The activated tap ability (1W, T: tap target creature) requires you to control a flying creature to activate — a conditional soft-lock that rewards white flying decks. EDHREC rank 28,366 is among the lowest in the set; it sees near-zero Commander play. Its competitive home, if any, is Pauper white flyers where the condition is easily met. [Scryfall]

Art by Bastien L. Deharme, a French illustrator who has done significant work for MTG in the religious-iconography and gothic spaces (Liesa, Forgotten Archangel; Vilis, Broker of Blood). The composition — backlit cleric with halo and raised hands — leans into Baroque religious painting conventions with deliberate gold-and-ivory palette. [Scryfall]
